references.title: Welcome to the new Linear
references.borrowed: Reduced visual noise across tabs, headers, filters, and panels while keeping information density high.
references.rejected: Did not turn replay into a product-management inbox or copy Linear’s product-specific chrome.

references.source: How we redesigned the Linear UI (part II)
references.title: How we redesigned the Linear UI (part II)
references.borrowed: Used the idea of the application chrome as the stable inverted-L frame so replay can intensify without changing the shell itself.
references.rejected: Did not adopt Linear’s broad redesign goal or its desktop-native assumptions as support workflow law.

references.title: Nested Layouts
references.borrowed: Persistent section chrome and child-route continuity informed the same-shell history, knowledge, and replay composition.
references.rejected: Did not mirror Next.js route architecture or add extra routing chrome outside the support route family.

references.title: New dashboard navigation available
references.borrowed: Consistent section navigation and efficient peer-route access informed the ticket rail and child-route tabs.
references.rejected: Did not use project switching, floating mobile navigation, or consumer dashboard patterns.

references.title: Data table
references.borrowed: Dense scanning, disciplined toolbar usage, narrow row grammar, and main-content placement informed the linked context and delta-review cards.
references.rejected: Did not reduce replay to a generic table or a batch-action operations console.

references.title: Typography
references.borrowed: Clear heading hierarchy and restrained density informed the replay header, blocker copy, and linked-context sectioning.
references.rejected: Did not import NHS public-brand styling or typography classes directly into the support shell.

references.title: Write the supporting content for your form
references.borrowed: Used short, in-context explanatory copy for blockers and restore guidance instead of tooltip-heavy or abstract helper text.
references.rejected: Did not add tooltip-only explanations or interface-teaching copy that would signal a weak workflow design.

references.title: Timeline
references.borrowed: The chronological vertical event structure informed the replay chronology and linked thread-history adaptation.
references.rejected: Did not treat the timeline as a neutral activity feed; masking, freeze, and restore law still dominate the support adaptation.
